today_summary: GCM26 spot printed $4,018.90, holding within the choppy $3,992–$4,070 five-day range. The chain stays call-heavy at a 0.54 P/C OI ratio (138,456 calls vs 74,716 puts), with the strongest floor at the $4,000 put wall (4,089 contracts) and resistance layering at $4,400 (4,133 ct), $4,700 (4,917 ct) and $4,800 (4,137 ct). Max pain sits at $4,795 — 19.3% above spot, a wide gap that keeps its pull weak this cycle. Net GEX within ±$200 of spot is -136.5, confirming dealers are short gamma right at the market with options volume today (2,942 calls / 2,573 puts) roughly half the week's average.
five_day_thesis: The dominant change this week is a fading put base: total put OI eroded 4.1% (77,953 → 74,716) while call OI held essentially flat (+0.7%), pulling the P/C OI ratio from 0.57 to 0.54 as puts were closed or expired rather than replaced — a structural, not cyclical, shift. Max pain drifted +$45 (4,750 → 4,795) even as spot round-tripped through the $3,992–$4,070 range for a net +$13.10 on the week, widening the max-pain gap to 19.3% above spot, the widest of the window. ATM IV tells a volatility round-trip: it spiked from 20.47% to 23.94% on 7/15 as spot dipped to $3,992, then fully compressed back to 20.47% by today — net unchanged over 5 sessions, confirming last week's dip was an event-driven vol spike that has already unwound completely. The $4,000 put wall and the $5,370 legacy call wall (largest single call strike overall, 8,008 ct, +33.6% OTM) have both been static for all 5 sessions with zero migration, while GEX has now printed negative every single day this week (window sum ranging -30.9 to -102.5) — a full week without one positive-gamma print. Today's volume is the tell that this is a lull, not a positioning shift: 2,942 calls and 2,573 puts traded vs the week's ~5,300/~5,200 averages, roughly half the flow.
what_changed: Put OI eroded 4.1% over 5 sessions (77,953 → 74,716) while call OI held flat (+0.7%), compressing P/C ratio 0.57 → 0.54.
Max pain drifted +$45 (4,750 → 4,795), now 19.3% above spot — the widest gap of the 5-day window.
ATM IV round-tripped 20.47% → 23.94% (7/15 spike) → back to 20.47% today — net unchanged but confirms a fully unwound vol event.
GEX printed negative for a 5th straight session (window sum -30.9 to -102.5) — no positive-gamma day this cycle.
$4,000 put wall and $5,370 legacy call wall both unchanged for all 5 sessions — zero wall migration.
Options volume roughly halved today (2,942 calls / 2,573 puts) vs the week's ~5,300/~5,200 average — a quiet session.
prediction: Bias is neutral-to-cautiously-bullish while spot holds above the $4,000 put wall; with GEX negative for a 5th straight session and -136.5 right at the market, a close below $4,000 should accelerate rather than stabilize, opening a fast move toward $3,900/$3,600. Above $4,018.9, expect grinding chop toward the $4,400 call cluster before the deeper $4,700/$4,800 resistance layer engages — max pain at $4,795 exerts only weak pull given the 19.3% distance. Invalidation: a daily close below $4,000.

day_trade_cta.bias_note: Holding above the $4,000 put wall keeps the tape constructive; negative GEX means downside below $4,000 accelerates fast rather than basing.
day_trade_cta.entry_note: Buy dips into the $4,000 put-wall / 1σ floor ($3,967) with confirmation; avoid chasing above $4,070, the top of the 5-day range.
day_trade_cta.pivot_note: Sustained trade above → path opens to $4,300 then the $4,400 call cluster. A closing loss of $4,000 fully activates the negative-GEX cascade — expect a fast slide to $3,900/$3,600.
day_trade_cta.stop_note: -0.9% from entry midpoint, just under the 1σ daily floor ($3,967.1) and the put wall itself.
day_trade_cta.target_note: T1 = $4,400 call-OI cluster (4,133 ct), take 50% off. T2 = $4,700 major call wall (4,917 ct), exit remaining.
day_trade_cta.risk_note: Dealers have been short gamma every session this week (window sum -30.9 to -102.5, today -136.5 within ±$200 of spot) — moves tend to accelerate, not mean-revert, once $4,000 breaks either direction. Size down 20-30%.
